content.question: Is the pedagogical value claim made by elite-school directors (that 6/8-year gimnáziums provide genuine quality differentiation beyond self-selection) empirically testable through matched-cohort or instrumental-variable analysis, or does the dispute reflect irreducible value disagreements about what 'quality' means?
content.reduction_path: Commission or fund a matched-cohort longitudinal study comparing 6/8-year gimnázium graduates and similar-ability students in non-selective or later-selective schools, controlling for family background, initial performance, and school resources; if effect sizes remain contested after such study, clarify whether disagreement is empirical or normative through structured expert deliberation with school directors and academic researchers.
